title: Pleurobotryum subulifolia
taxonomy: Pleurobotryum subulifolia belongs to the phylum Chlorophyta, a group of green algae. This species is classified under the order Siphonales, which is a group of filamentous green algae. The class Ulvophyceae is also associated with Pleurobotryum subulifolia, as it is a part of this class. The family Pleurocladaceae is the family under which this species is classified. The genus Pleurobotryum itself is also a part of the family Pleurocladaceae.
constituents: Pleurobotryum subulifolia contains various active constituents that contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The exact composition of Pleurobotryum subulifolia's active constituents may vary depending on factors such as the plant's habitat and growing conditions. Further research is necessary to fully understand the effects and potential benefits of these constituents. The study of Pleurobotryum subulifolia's active constituents continues to be an area of interest for researchers in the field of botany and pharmacology.
